subject: Accounting
title: The Genius of Double Entry
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Genius of Double Entry usable.

hook.text: Luca Pacioli published the double-entry system in 1494. We still use it today—unchanged.

buildup.text: Double entry records every transaction twice—once as a debit and once as a credit.

discovery.text: This ensures the books always balance. If debits don't equal credits, something is wrong.

twist.text: The system is self-checking—errors reveal themselves because the equation breaks.

climax.text: Over 500 years later, every accounting system on Earth still follows Pacioli's elegant design.

punchline.text: 500 years old and still the foundation of all accounting.

quiz.question: Who published the double-entry bookkeeping system?
